title: uuf2dbl
html:
uuf2dbl

Copy a floating-point value from a basic parameter or entity field to a double program variable.

long uuf2dbl (UHACT hOpr, int seqNr, double* pDblVal);

Parameters

hOpr
The handle to the operation or entity parameter
seqNr
The sequence number of the basic parameter or entity field (sequence numbers start with 1)
pDblVal
(Output) The double value

Return Values

For more information, see the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic.

Use

Library: ucall

Description

uuf2dbl copies the value from a basic parameter or entity field to a double program variable

uuf2dbl converts the data from Uniface internal format to C double format. A conversion error will result if the data in the parameter or field is not compatible with the double format. If you need to access the data in Uniface internal format, use uprmgets rather than uuf2dbl.

When you use this function to retrieve data from a Uniface field of type float, keep in mind that the Uniface float format allows for larger data than the C double format. So it is possible that you might see truncation or rounding.

Example: 3GL copy functions

The following examples demonstrate the use of the functions ubin2uf, udbl2uf, ulong2uf, uuf2bin, uuf2dbl, and uuf2long.

The service TESTSVC contains two operations: GETP which has three output parameters of types numeric, float, and raw, and SETP which has three input parameters of types numeric, float, and raw. These two services are defined as follows:

operation getp
params
numeric pnum : out;
float pflt : out;
raw praw : out;
endparams

pnum = 147483648
pflt = 3.1415926535897
fileload/raw "d:\temp\db2cli.gif", praw
end

operation setp
params
numeric pnum : in;
float pflt : in;
raw praw : in;
endparams

putmess "pnum = %%pnum"
putmess "pflt = %%pflt"
putmess "praw = %%praw"
end

The following 3GL code will activate GETP, retrieve the three values, change them a bit, then pass them to SETP, which prints them to the message frame.

#include <h3gl.h>
test_params ()
{
UHACT hEnv, hIn; /* Environment and instance handle */
UHACT hOp1, hOp2; /* Operation handles */
long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */
UBYTE* pBin; /* Pointer to binary parameter data */
long binLen;
long longVal;
double dblVal;

ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */
uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"TESTSVC", 0, 0,
UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr); /* Instantiate TESTSVC */

uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"GETP", 0, &Op1);/* Instantiate operation GETP*/
uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"SETP", 0, &Op2);/* Instantiate operation SETP*/

uopract (hOp1, &actStatus, &procErr);/* Activate operation GETP */

uuf2long(hOp1, 1, &longVal); /* Get long value */
uuf2dbl (hOp1, 2, &dblVal); /* Get double value */
uuf2bin (hOp1, 3, &pBin, &binLen); /* Get pointer to binary data*/

ulong2uf(hOp2, 1, longVal / 2); /* Put long value */
udbl2uf (hOp2, 2, dblVal / 2); /* Put double value */
ubin2uf (hOp2, 3, pBin, 20); /* Put 20 bytes of binary data*/

uopract (hOp2, &actStatus, &procErr);/* Activate operation GETP */

uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */
unifree (hOp1, pBin); /* Free the binary data */
ufreeh (&Op1); /* Free GETP operation handle*/
ufreeh (&Op2); /* Free SETP operation handle*/
ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */
ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */
}

Here is what is SETP prints to the message frame:

pnum = 73741824
pflt = 1.57079632679485e+000
praw = GIF89a|R{$q{#|i{!{!{!{!{!{F{!{!{!
Related Links
  • Call-In API Return Codes

    Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise.

  • udbl2uf

    Copy double floating point data to a basic parameter or entity field.

  • uuf2bin

    Return a pointer to the binary data in a basic parameter or entity field.
